The launch of the Innovation Task Force, announced by Chairman Michael S. Selig, marks a proactive approach by the agency to establish clear standards for firms using new technologies, including cryptocurrency, artificial intelligence, and event contracts. This move is expected to support US developers, reduce legal uncertainty, and foster responsible innovation while maintaining market integrity.

### [Senate Eyes CLARITY Act Markup This Month as Banks, Crypto Continue Stablecoin Yield Talks](https://cryptonewsnft.com/reviews/senate-eyes-clarity-act-markup-this-month-as-banks)

> The cryptocurrency industry is eagerly awaiting the Senate Banking Committee's markup of the CLARITY Act, which is expected to take place by the end of March. The Act, which has already passed the House with a significant majority of 294-134 in July 2025, aims to provide clarity on the regulatory framework for stablecoins and other digital assets. However, negotiations have stalled in the Senate Banking Committee over the issue of stablecoin yield limits, with banks and crypto firms remaining split on the matter.
